description: The embedding model or index does not fit the real query distribution, so relevant passages never enter the candidate set.
body: Failure mode: a default embedding or an approximate index is adopted without measuring it on real queries, so semantically relevant or exact-term passages are missed and recall silently drops. Detection signals: embedding chosen without a recall measurement; exact-term queries (names, codes) miss; an approximate index used with no recall check; mixed embeddings in one index. Do not assume a default embedding fits or that approximate recall is adequate. Safer fallback: evaluate candidate embeddings on a labeled query set from real traffic, choose dense/sparse/hybrid by query type, tune the index to the recall budget, and re-embed consistently on model change.
